title: JIT infrastructure — Cranelift integration and compilation pipeline
purpose: The JIT compiler transforms hot BEAM functions into native machine code via Cranelift. This brief sets up the compilation pipeline: required Cranelift dependencies, IR builder abstraction, the tiered execution model (interpret first, compile after N calls), and the background compilation pathway via dirty CPU threads. Per D2, Cranelift is always-on and not feature gated.
task: Add Cranelift as a required dependency, define the JitCompiler struct that translates BEAM instruction sequences into Cranelift IR and emits callable native code, implement a per-function call profiler that triggers compilation after a configurable threshold, and route compilation work to the dirty CPU thread pool. This brief delivers the scaffold only — no opcode translation (B-131+). The jit/ module root follows the project convention: pub mod + pub use only, no logic.
boundaries: SHALL NOT implement any opcode translation — only the pipeline scaffold (opcode translation is B-131 through B-137)
SHALL NOT block any scheduler thread during compilation — all JIT work runs on dirty CPU threads
SHALL NOT install compiled code into any cache in this brief — cache is B-133
SHALL NOT emit GC safepoints in this brief — safepoints are B-134
verification: cargo tree -p beamr shows Cranelift crates
No JIT feature gates exist in the codebase
No Cranelift types appear in beamr's public API
